data.body:
PASS-4 COACH + UNIFIED-SCORE + ADVISORY-CAP work on the plan-review gate (epic 5fd2). Summary of the arc.

== 1. Pass-4 affirmative COACH (added to 5fd2 + new child 75a9 crusty-herb-stand) ==
A single-turn, structured-output LLM call (NOT an agent) running after Pass-3 over the surviving advisory findings,
priming the caller with a productive NEXT MOVE per finding (beyond returning findings). Maps each finding to a move
from a fixed, project-extensible MOVE REGISTRY (moves 1-9 + 11 propagate-revisions-to-children + 12 generalize-the-
finding; move 10 reference-class cut) and emits {move_id, subject, finding_refs}; coaching prose rendered
DETERMINISTICALLY from each move's LOCKED template (LLM authors no free prose). Design A/B/D approved; C re-derived
for LLM RECIPIENTS (human-pedagogy dropped after research — LLMs can't self-correct from reflection (Huang/DeepMind),
sycophancy makes them over-adopt confident wrong fixes, lost-in-the-middle): C1 actionable-guidance-not-executable;
C2 reference findings by id (no restate); C3 select-from-registry + fill-subject; C4 cap/cluster/front-load. The
coach is a single-turn structured-output call by design (no tooling).

== 2. Self-review of the coach (log plum-strut-reek/jibe-ditty-ant) found + fixed 3 real ones ==
- ADVISORY: C1 was NOT enforced "by construction" — `subject` is a free-text field the template interpolates, so a
  solution could be smuggled in. FIX: a SUBJECT VALIDATOR (bounded noun-phrase; code/imperative/solution rejected).
- finding-`id` was minted by no child (75a9<->db7b circular). FIX: the orchestrator ca03 MINTS it (sole owner);
  db7b + 75a9 CONSUME it. (Exactly the move-#11 propagation gap the coach exists to coach.)
- the "Review structure — three-pass" section omitted Pass-4. FIX: added it.
Also filed bug `slim-fetch-ledge` (ed2b): `rebar show` intermittently returns empty stdout under concurrent
background auto-push (REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=always); confirmed by it working reliably with push=off.

== 3. Unified priority SCORE (design decision) ==
Validity and impact are BOTH LLM-judged spectrums (neither absolute): a low-validity/high-impact finding may warrant
surfacing (confirm it's not real); a high-validity/low-impact one may not need a remedy to ship. So they are COMBINED
into ONE deterministic priority score (NOT two separate gates), used for both the block threshold and the advisory
ranking. (Supersedes an earlier split-axis proposal.)

== 4. Cap-dilution EXPERIMENT (workflow; advisory-cap-dilution v1 -> v2) ==
Tested H1 = "as the non-blocking list grows, an LLM reviser ignores the high-value findings". v1 hit a CEILING
(seeded defects too easy; no-findings floor 0.81; all list-sizes = 1.0 — useless). v2 (finding-dependent HARD defects
= TOCTOU/lost-update/unsafe-default/missing-failure-path; STRICT grader; L up to 32; null-safe agg):
  floor (no findings) = 0.41  ->  high-value remediation: L4=0.99, L12=0.96, L20=0.98, L32=0.91.
=> findings clearly MATTER (floor 0.41 -> ~0.99 surfaced), but NO material LLM dilution of high-value action up to
~20 (only a slight, noisy dip at 32). Refutes the strong "large list -> ignore all" claim FOR A STRONG SINGLE-PASS
REVISER IN THIS RANGE; consistent with the literature (Greptile 19% was HUMANS; IFScale collapse is at hundreds of
items). The model triages substantive findings, and "addressing" a flagged PLAN issue is cheap.
LIMITATIONS (valuable but INCONCLUSIVE ALONE): only one (strong) model — did NOT test a smaller model (Sonnet); did
NOT run under CONTEXT PRESSURE (a common cause of agents shortcutting / ignoring feedback); 1 model x 2 plans x R=6.
Artifacts: workflow scripts advisory-cap-dilution(-v2) in the session workflows dir (not committed).

== 5. DECISION + REVISIONS APPLIED (this turn; epic + children, all gate-pass) ==
- CAP = 10, CONFIG-DRIVEN (owned by 55de), TUNED later from real-world dogfood data — not a validated constant.
- The cap = top-N ADVISORY by the unified score; advisory overflow -> REVIEW_RESULT sidecar (not surfaced). Volume
  (the cap), not labeling, is the lever that preserves LLM action (research-grounded).
- THE CAP NEVER DROPS BLOCKING FINDINGS: blocking is EXEMPT; if blocking count > cap, ALL blocking findings are
  returned. The cap can never weaken a block.
- ITERATION-FATIGUE rationale encoded: LLMs frustrated across review rounds (round 3/4 keeps surfacing NEW findings)
  tend to FORCE PAST a blocked gate rather than keep addressing feedback; a bounded prioritized convergent advisory
  surface + never-drop-blocking mitigate this.
- Tickets revised: 5fd2 (cap + unified score + iteration-fatigue + experiment & limitations), 487d Pass-3 aggregator
  (unified score + cap mechanics + blocking-exempt), 55de config schema (+= advisory cap), 75a9 coach (input = the
  capped advisory set; blocking surfaced uncapped).

NEXT (not now): tune the cap from dogfood data; the deferred experiment arms (smaller model + context pressure) would
firm up the dilution picture; implement per the children when work begins (registry/orchestrator first).
